meta.description: Architect command E2E tests. Validates the /dev:architect rename from /dev:plan, complexity triage, plan mode integration, task-management skill loading, and GTD plugin integration. Tests real developer scenarios across simple/moderate/complex architecture tasks.
meta.notes: v1.0: Tests rename routing, complexity triage, plan mode usage, task creation patterns, GTD awareness. All tests use prompts that stop after early phases to keep execution fast (< 2 min each).

test_cases.description: Verify /dev:architect loads the renamed command (not old /dev:plan)
test_cases.prompt: /dev:architect Design a caching layer for our user service. Stop after showing the complexity triage result — do NOT proceed to architecture design. Just show the triage assessment and what mode you would recommend.

test_cases.description: Verify /dev:plan no longer exists as a command
test_cases.prompt: /dev:plan Design a user service. Just tell me if this command loaded successfully or not. Do not proceed with any design work.

test_cases.description: Simple task should be triaged as Simple complexity — adding Redis caching to one service
test_cases.prompt: /dev:architect Add Redis caching to the user profile endpoint. Only do the complexity triage step (Phase 0). Show the triage result and stop. Do NOT proceed to requirements or design.

test_cases.description: Complex task should be triaged as Complex — real-time collaboration engine spans multiple systems
test_cases.prompt: /dev:architect Design a real-time collaborative document editing system with conflict resolution, presence indicators, offline support, and multi-device sync. Only do the complexity triage step. Show the triage result, complexity signals you detected, and what approach you recommend. Do NOT proceed further.

test_cases.description: Moderate/complex task should trigger plan mode reasoning (EnterPlanMode)
test_cases.prompt: /dev:architect Design a multi-tenant authorization system with role-based access control, tenant isolation, and audit logging. This involves cross-cutting concerns across 3 subsystems. Run the complexity triage, then if moderate or complex, enter plan mode to reason about the architecture. Stop after plan mode reasoning — do NOT proceed to requirements.

test_cases.description: Architect command should create phase tasks upfront following task-management skill patterns
test_cases.prompt: /dev:architect Design a REST API for a bookmark manager app. This is a simple, well-understood problem. Run triage, then create the phase tasks and show them. Stop after creating tasks — do NOT start the actual architecture work.

test_cases.description: With GTD plugin active (.claude/gtd/tasks.json exists), architect should detect it and suggest /gtd:work
test_cases.prompt:
First run these setup steps:
1. mkdir -p .claude/gtd
2. echo '{"version":"1.0","lastReview":null,"activeTaskId":null,"tasks":[]}' > .claude/gtd/tasks.json

Now run: /dev:architect Design a notification service. Only do the initialization phase. Check for GTD plugin and report whether it was detected. Then stop — do NOT proceed with design.

After reporting, clean up: rm -rf .claude/gtd

test_cases.description: Without GTD plugin, architect should use native tasks and suggest GTD installation
test_cases.prompt:
First ensure GTD store does not exist: rm -rf .claude/gtd

Now run: /dev:architect Design a simple key-value cache. Only do the initialization phase. Check for GTD plugin and report whether it was found or not. Then stop — do NOT proceed with design.

test_cases.description: Open-ended question should auto-infer brainstorm mode and load brainstorming skill
test_cases.prompt: /dev:architect How should we handle real-time notifications in our app? Only do the mode selection step. Tell me which mode you auto-inferred (Architecture design vs Brainstorm first) and why. Do NOT actually run the brainstorming workflow.

test_cases.description: Clear system description should auto-infer architecture design mode
test_cases.prompt: /dev:architect Design a PostgreSQL schema for a multi-tenant SaaS billing system with subscription tiers, usage tracking, and invoice generation. Only do the mode selection step. Tell me which mode you auto-inferred and why. Do NOT proceed with design.

test_cases.description: When previous architecture session exists for same topic, should detect retry and recommend /team
test_cases.prompt:
First create a fake previous session:
1. mkdir -p ai-docs/sessions/dev-arch-auth-20260320-100000-abcd1234
2. echo '# Auth Architecture v1' > ai-docs/sessions/dev-arch-auth-20260320-100000-abcd1234/architecture.md

Now run: /dev:architect Design the auth system. Only do the complexity triage. Check for previous sessions with similar topic. Report whether a retry was detected and what you recommend. Stop after triage.

Clean up: rm -rf ai-docs/sessions/dev-arch-auth-20260320-100000-abcd1234

test_cases.description: Architect command should create session directory under ai-docs/sessions/dev-arch-*
test_cases.prompt:
/dev:architect Design a URL shortener service. Run only Phase 0 (Initialize) — create the session directory and detect the stack. Report the session path. Stop after Phase 0.

After reporting the session path, clean up: remove the session directory you created.

test_cases.description: Verify task-management skill is loaded (architect should reference GTD detection pattern)
test_cases.prompt: /dev:architect Design a simple CRUD API for notes. Before starting any architecture work, demonstrate that you loaded the task-management skill by: (1) checking for GTD plugin, (2) checking for stale tasks. Report what you found, then stop.
